message: Jake
Yuriko
Ash
The room looked like an ordinary bathroom from any angle, except for the coffin that was placed there.
It looks like an ordinary bathroom, right? Why were they hiding this in such a complicated way?
And that coffin really seems to scream, 'Look at me!'
A bit creepy.
I guess it's good that it's daytime.
The boss is such a coward.
Shall we take a little peek?
Yeah, but I have a bad feeling about this.
A trap, maybe?
The three of us cautiously approached the coffin and opened its lid.
What is this?
Inside the coffin were body parts presumed to belong to previous victims, neatly arranged.
This.
Is this real?
Probably.
They're likely treated with special processing to keep them from rotting.
Ugh.
I, I can't.
No way.
She went back to the other room.
Everything was preserved neatly, though they had a bit of a dried-out feel.
They must have undergone some kind of preservation or special plastination treatment.
So this means the owner of this room is the one who committed those murders.
Yeah. They probably committed multiple murders while being stuck in New York for film shooting.
But what could be the purpose of this?
Who knows?
Shouldn't we report this to the police?
Yeah, that seems like a good idea.
If the owner of this place is identified, we'll know who the culprit is. Let's investigate this further.
Nice to meet you.
We left the scene as it was and contacted the police.
However, if we talk about those involved in that movie who are still alive and attended the director's party at the New York apartment,
it’s only Director Fabar, Jennifer, and Meryl in prison.
There are only those three.
So, whose name was the apartment under?
It was a woman named Juliet Furlong.
Juliet Furlong? I don't know anyone by that name.
No, you do know her. Juliet Furlong is Jennifer herself.
Jennifer?
So, you’re saying she’s the culprit?
What!?
It's also possible she was framed, but I think it's almost certainly her.
No way.
That’s ridiculous.
You can't be serious.
